item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-034","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response immediately refuses adding a third fallback and identifies the existing two fallback branches plus continued instability as a red-state signal. It restricts action to locating the upstream instability/root cause and suggests consolidating or deleting an existing fallback if they mask the same issue. It does not design or add a third fallback, except to say it should only be considered after a distinct reproducible failure mode is proven."}
